title: agent-runtime: observability gaps - structured logging, metrics export, distributed tracing
summary: Issue #591 tracks production observability hardening for packages/agent-runtime. Current daemon logging is append-only JSONL with no levels, filtering, rotation, or sampling. Health snapshots compute average latency but not P50/P95/P99. Metrics are computed locally but not exported through Prometheus, CloudWatch, or diagnostics surfaces. Telemetry is in-memory only and lacks OTLP export, W3C trace context, and cross-process correlation IDs. The issue also calls out missing diagnostics endpoints for /health, /metrics, /config, and /queue.

relatedIssues.reason: Transport-mux session-aware proxy is a likely distributed trace propagation boundary.

triageFindings: packages/agent-runtime/src/daemon/daemonLog.ts only appends JSON lines with timestamp, event, and optional data to daemon.jsonl.
packages/agent-runtime/src/observability/health.ts computes counters and avgEffectLatencyMs; packages/agent-runtime/src/observability/types.ts has no P50/P95/P99 latency fields.
packages/agent-runtime/src/telemetry/provider.ts implements InMemoryTelemetryProvider with flush and drain but no OTLP/file/console exporter implementation.
packages/agent-runtime/src/telemetry/types.ts has OpenTelemetry-aligned span types but no W3C traceparent boundary or cross-process correlation helpers.
packages/agent-runtime/src/observability/webhooks.ts builds webhook events from health snapshots but is not a metrics export or diagnostics API surface.
docs/agent-layer-gaps.md records the same medium-priority L5 gaps under no structured logging, no health percentiles, no metrics export, no distributed tracing, and limited daemon tests.

acceptanceFocus: Daemon logging remains backward compatible with existing JSONL readers while adding log levels, filtering, rotation, optional deterministic sampling, and schema/version metadata.

Health metrics preserve avgEffectLatencyMs and add deterministic p50EffectLatencyMs, p95EffectLatencyMs, and p99EffectLatencyMs fields with tests for empty, single, ordered, and unordered latency samples.
Metrics export includes a Prometheus-compatible text surface or endpoint with stable metric names and bounded labels.
Diagnostics surfaces expose safe /health, /metrics, /config, and /queue behavior with local-only or explicitly enabled defaults and test-covered secret redaction.
Telemetry supports W3C traceparent parse/serialize, propagated parent context, correlation IDs across runtime boundaries, and optional exporters while preserving in-memory/no-op defaults.
Exporter failures or missing external telemetry services do not prevent daemon startup or normal local runtime behavior.
Public exports are intentional and documented enough for downstream packages to use without importing private source paths.
Implementation coordinates with #578 and #599 by exposing integration points without implementing cost tracking or transport-mux session-aware proxy scope.
nonGoals: Do not implement queue persistence, dead letter queues, crash recovery, or supervisor/systemd integration.
Do not implement #578 cost tracking beyond exposing metrics integration points useful to it.
Do not implement #599 transport-mux session-aware proxy behavior beyond trace context/correlation primitives in agent-runtime.
Do not require Prometheus, CloudWatch, OTLP collectors, or any remote telemetry backend for daemon startup.
Do not remove timestamp, event, or data fields from daemon JSONL entries.
Do not generate shell-oriented process subtasks for this repository workflow.
qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse audit is rendered before new observability infrastructure is added.
Regression tests are written before implementation changes for logging, health percentiles, diagnostics/metrics, and tracing/exporters.
Targeted agent-runtime tests pass for daemon logging, observability health/diagnostics, and telemetry trace/export behavior.
Full agent-runtime test suite passes or unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with exact evidence.
Security review confirms diagnostics /config output and logs redact secrets and do not expose private run data by default.
Compatibility review confirms existing JSONL readers, public package exports, and in-memory telemetry defaults remain compatible.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #591, comments, labels, and related issue references to lock the behavioral spec and scope boundaries.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Render repo-required reuse-audit findings before adding logging, metrics, diagnostics, or tracing infrastructure.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime observability trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Map current daemon logging, health snapshots, webhook alerts, daemon queue/status state, telemetry providers, package exports, and tests.

plannedPhases.name: Observability design
plannedPhases.purpose: Define compatible logging, health percentile, diagnostics/metrics, and trace propagation/export slices with safe defaults.

plannedPhases.name: Regression coverage
plannedPhases.purpose: Add failing tests for each reported observability gap before implementation changes.

plannedPhases.name: Incremental implementation
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement structured logging policy, health percentiles, diagnostics/Prometheus surfaces, and W3C trace/export primitives in small slices.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted and full agent-runtime gates, iterating up to three attempts if tests or review find regressions.

plannedPhases.name: SRE/security/compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review for secret leakage, unsafe network defaults, metrics cardinality, exporter failure isolation, JSONL compatibility, and package API drift.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm acceptance criteria, verification evidence, review approval, changed files, release-note candidate, and follow-up boundaries.

breakpoints.when: Only if implementation discovers a real maintainer decision is needed, such as enabling diagnostics over HTTP by default, adding a new mandatory telemetry dependency, or choosing between incompatible trace propagation APIs.
